data.query: employee data location and processing rules

citations.passage: All company data processing shall occur exclusively on US-domiciled servers. VPN access restricted to US IP ranges.

citations.passage: No employee data may be transferred to non-US cloud providers without CISO written approval.

data.conclusion: Validation passed: three-way structural impossibility confirmed — US-only server mandate vs. India-resident jurisdiction vs. global location permission.

data.topic: employee data location and processing rules

data.conclusion: Affects Bangalore engineering team (240 FTEs) and all EU remote workers. DPDP effective July 1, 2026 — 19 days from policy review date.

citations.passage: Personal data of Indian-resident employees must be processed within Indian jurisdiction.
citations.topic: employee data location and processing rules

citations.passage: All company data processing shall occur exclusively on US-domiciled servers.
citations.topic: employee data location and processing rules

data.conclusion: Three policies make simultaneous compliance structurally impossible. DPDP enforcement begins July 1, 2026 — 19 days. Non-compliance risks Indian Data Protection Board investigation and GDPR cross-border transfer violations.

assessment.reasoning: IT Security mandates US-only server processing. The DPDP Directive requires Indian-jurisdiction processing for Indian-resident employees effective July 1, 2026. HR Remote Work permits those employees to work globally — meaning their data is processed at their location. All three rules apply to the same 240-person Bangalore engineering population simultaneously. They cannot all be satisfied. DPDP enforcement begins in 19 days.

supporting_evidence.topic: employee data location and processing rules

supporting_evidence.passage: Employees may work from any global location without prior approval.
supporting_evidence.topic: employee data location and processing rules

potential_consequences: Indian Data Protection Board investigation under DPDP Act 2023 (effective July 1, 2026 — 19 days).
GDPR cross-border transfer violation for EU employees.
Engineering data pipeline redesign required before deadline.

data.conclusion: Implement geo-fenced data processing: Indian-resident employee data processed on India-region servers; EU employee data on EU-region servers. IT Security Policy §4.2 must be amended to recognize jurisdictional carve-outs for DPDP and GDPR. CISO and Legal must co-sign the amended policy before June 30, 2026.

data.title: Data residency: structural impossibility
data.affected: Bangalore engineering team (240 FTEs) and EU remote workers. DPDP effective July 1, 2026.
data.resolution: Implement geo-fenced data processing with Indian-region and EU-region server carve-outs. Amend IT Security Policy §4.2 to recognize jurisdictional exceptions. CISO and Legal must co-sign before June 30, 2026.

data.title: Data residency: structural impossibility
data.affected: Bangalore engineering team (240 FTEs) and EU remote workers. DPDP effective July 1, 2026.
data.resolution: Implement geo-fenced data processing with Indian-region and EU-region server carve-outs. Amend IT Security Policy §4.2 to recognize jurisdictional exceptions. CISO and Legal must co-sign before June 30, 2026.

citations.topic: employee data location and processing rules

citations.passage: Employees may work from any global location without prior approval. Data access follows the employee’s location.
citations.topic: employee data location and processing rules

risk_assessment.reasoning: IT Security mandates US-only server processing. DPDP requires Indian-jurisdiction processing effective July 1, 2026. HR Remote Work permits global work. DPDP enforcement begins in 19 days.
potential_consequences: Indian Data Protection Board investigation under DPDP Act 2023.

data.conclusion: IT Security Policy §4.2 mandates US-only servers. DPDP Compliance Directive §3.1 mandates Indian-jurisdiction processing for Indian employees effective July 1, 2026. HR Remote Work §6.2 permits those employees to work from any location. All three apply simultaneously to 240 Bangalore engineers. Structurally impossible to satisfy all three.
